diagrams.title: Autoboxing and Unboxing Flow
diagrams.description: Diagram showing the conversion between primitive types and wrapper classes in Java
elements: Left side: Primitive types (int, double, boolean, char)
Middle: Wrapper Classes (Integer, Double, Boolean, Character)
Arrows show automatic conversion by compiler
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the bidirectional conversion between primitives and wrapper objects

diagrams.title: Integer Cache Memory Model
diagrams.description: Shows how Integer objects are cached for values -128 to 127
elements: Inside cache: Multiple references pointing to same objects
Outside cache: Each Integer value (128+) gets separate object
Memory heap showing distinct object locations
diagrams.purpose: Explain why == works unexpectedly with Integer objects

diagrams.title: Autoboxing in Collections
diagrams.description: Shows how ArrayList handles primitive int values
elements: Arrows showing: int 10 → autoboxes to → Integer(10) stored in list
Code: list.add(10) → compiler converts to list.add(Integer.valueOf(10))
Retrieval: Integer obj = list.get(0) → unboxes to int when used in arithmetic
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate practical use of autoboxing in Java Collections
